heading: Restitution
label: 4215 Restitution
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to section 1989b–4 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

children.heading: Location and payment of eligible individuals
children.label: a Location and payment of eligible individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (7), the Attorney General shall, subject to the availability of funds appropriated to the Fund for such purpose, pay out of the Fund to each eligible individual the sum of $20,000, unless such individual refuses, in the manner described in paragraph (5), to accept the payment.

children.heading: Location of eligible individuals
children.label: 2 Location of eligible individuals
children.text: The Attorney General shall identify and locate, without requiring any application for payment and using records already in the possession of the United States Government, each eligible individual. The Attorney General should use funds and resources available to the Attorney General, including those described in subsection (c), to attempt to complete such identification and location within 12 months after . Any eligible individual may notify the Attorney General that such individual is an eligible individual, and may provide documentation therefor. The Attorney General shall designate an officer or employee to whom such notification and documentation may be sent, shall maintain a list of all individuals who submit such notification and documentation, and shall, subject to the availability of funds appropriated for such purpose, encourage, through a public awareness campaign, each eligible individual to submit his or her current address to such officer or employee. To the extent that resources referred to in the second sentence of this paragraph are not sufficient to complete the identification and location of all eligible individuals, there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for such purpose. In any case, the identification and location of all eligible individuals shall be completed within 12 months after the appropriation of funds under the preceding sentence. Failure to be identified and located by the end of the 12-month period specified in the preceding sentence shall not preclude an eligible individual from receiving payment under this section.August 10, 1988

children.heading: Benefit of the doubt
children.label: 3 Benefit of the doubt
children.text: When, after consideration of all evidence and relevant material for determining whether an individual is an eligible individual, there is an approximate balance of positive and negative evidence regarding the merits of an issue material to the determination of eligibility, the benefit of the doubt in resolving each such issue shall be given to such individual.

children.label: A
children.text: acceptance of payment under this section shall be in full satisfaction of all claims against the United States arising out of acts described in , andsection 4218(2)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: each eligible individual who does not refuse, in the manner described in paragraph (5), to accept payment under this section within 18 months after receiving such written notice shall be deemed to have accepted payment for purposes of paragraph (6).

children.heading: Effect of refusal to accept payment
children.label: 5 Effect of refusal to accept payment
children.text: If an eligible individual refuses, in a written document filed with the Attorney General, to accept any payment under this section, the amount of such payment shall remain in the Fund and no payment may be made under this section to such individual at any time after such refusal.

children.heading: Payment in full settlement of claims against the United States
children.label: 6 Payment in full settlement of claims against the United States
children.text: The acceptance of payment by an eligible individual under this section shall be in full satisfaction of all claims against the United States arising out of acts described in . This paragraph shall apply to any eligible individual who does not refuse, in the manner described in paragraph (5), to accept payment under this section within 18 months after receiving the notification from the Attorney General referred to in paragraph (4).section 4218(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion of certain individuals
children.label: 7 Exclusion of certain individuals
children.text: No payment may be made under this section to any individual who, after , accepts payment pursuant to an award of a final judgment or a settlement on a claim against the United States for acts described in , or to any surviving spouse, child, or parent of such individual to whom paragraph (8) applies.September 1, 1987section 4218(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Payments in the case of deceased persons
children.label: 8 Payments in the case of deceased persons
i
children.text: If the eligible individual is survived by a spouse who is living at the time of payment, such payment shall be made to such surviving spouse.

children.label: ii
children.text: If there is no surviving spouse described in clause (i), such payment shall be made in equal shares to all children of the eligible individual who are living at the time of payment.

children.label: iii
children.text: If there is no surviving spouse described in clause (i) and if there are no children described in clause (ii), such payment shall be made in equal shares to the parents of the eligible individual who are living at the time of payment.

children.label: B
children.text: After the death of an eligible individual, this subsection and subsections (c) and (f) shall apply to the individual or individuals specified in subparagraph (A) to whom payment under this section will be made, to the same extent as such subsections apply to the eligible individual.

children.label: i
children.text: the “spouse” of an eligible individual means a wife or husband of an eligible individual who was married to that eligible individual for at least 1 year immediately before the death of the eligible individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: a “child” of an eligible individual includes a recognized natural child, a stepchild who lived with the eligible individual in a regular parent-child relationship, and an adopted child; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a “parent” of an eligible individual includes fathers and mothers through adoption.

children.heading: Order of payments
children.label: b Order of payments
children.text: The Attorney General shall endeavor to make payments under this section to eligible individuals in the order of date of birth (with the oldest individual on (or, if applicable, that individual’s survivors under paragraph (8)) receiving full payment first), until all eligible individuals have received payment in full.August 10, 1988

children.heading: Resources for locating eligible individuals
children.label: c Resources for locating eligible individuals
children.text: In attempting to locate any eligible individual, the Attorney General may use any facility or resource of any public or nonprofit organization or any other record, document, or information that may be made available to the Attorney General.

children.heading: Administrative costs not paid from the Fund
children.label: d Administrative costs not paid from the Fund
children.text: No costs incurred by the Attorney General in carrying out this section shall be paid from the Fund or set off against, or otherwise deducted from, any payment under this section to any eligible individual.

children.heading: Termination of duties of Attorney General
children.label: e Termination of duties of Attorney General
children.text: The duties of the Attorney General under this section shall cease 180 days after the Fund terminates.

children.heading: Clarification of treatment of payments under other laws
children.label: f Clarification of treatment of payments under other laws

children.text: shall be treated for purposes of the internal revenue laws of the United States as damages for human suffering; and

shall not be included as income or resources for purposes of determining eligibility to receive benefits described in or available under any other law administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, or for purposes of determining the amount of such benefits.section 3803(c)(2)(C) of title 31

children.heading: Liability of United States limited to amount in Fund
children.label: g Liability of United States limited to amount in Fund

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: An eligible individual may be paid under this section only from amounts in the Fund.

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: 2 Coordination with other provisions
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall authorize the payment to an eligible individual by the United States Government of any amount authorized by this section from any source other than the Fund.

children.heading: Order in which unpaid claims to be paid
children.label: 3 Order in which unpaid claims to be paid
children.text: If at any time the Fund has insufficient funds to pay all eligible individuals at such time, such eligible individuals shall, to the extent permitted under paragraph (1), be paid in full in the order specified in subsection (b).

children.heading: Review by the Court of Federal Claims
children.label: 1 Review by the Court of Federal Claims
children.text: A claimant may seek judicial review of a denial of compensation under this section solely in the United States Court of Federal Claims, which shall review the denial upon the administrative record and shall hold unlawful and set aside the denial if it is found to be arbitrary, capricious, an abuse of discretion, or otherwise not in accordance with law.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: This subsection shall apply only to any claim filed in court on or after .September 27, 1992
